extra.reasoning: rec_drug_b is set true only by r1 (IF diabetes). rec_drug_a is set true only by r3, whose condition is the conjunction diabetes AND low_ejection_fraction AND prior_gi_bleed AND hypertension AND renal_impairment AND g6pd_deficiency. Because r3's condition includes diabetes as a conjunct, any input that fires r3 (making rec_drug_a true) necessarily also satisfies r1's condition (making rec_drug_b true). Priorities do not help: r1 targets rec_drug_b and r3 targets rec_drug_a, so they never contend for the same output — priority/order tie-breaking only applies among rules writing the same output. Thus both outputs are set true simultaneously. Setting the six conjuncts of r3 to true (and the unrelated qt_prolongation/heart_failure/hepatic_impairment to false, since they only affect rec_drug_c) yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true, jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property.
